name: telemetry-otel
description: OpenTelemetry instrumentation surface. Wraps `keepsuit/laravel-opentelemetry` under the hood — gains framework-native auto-instrumentation of HTTP, DB queries, queue jobs, cache operations, view rendering, and Guzzle out-calls with zero application changes. Adds attribute-driven manual instrumentation: `#[AsSpan]`, `#[AsCounter]`, `#[AsHistogram]`, `#[AsGauge]`, `#[AsContext]`, `#[AsBaggage]`. Attributes discovered at build-time and registered with the SDK; runtime uses vanilla method calls. Exports OTLP to any collector (Grafana Tempo/Mimir, DataDog Agent, Honeycomb, Signoz, self-hosted). Not a data store — fires and forgets.

wraps.rationale: Laravel-native OTel package with turn-key auto-instrumentation. Alternative (`open-telemetry/sdk` + `open-telemetry/exporter-otlp` directly) requires ~500 lines of provider glue we would have to maintain. keepsuit is a thin composition layer over the official SDK — no lock-in.

http_server.description: Every inbound HTTP request → root span. Method, route, status, duration, request_id (from foundation).

http_client.description: Every Guzzle / HTTP facade outcall → client span with URL, method, response status, duration.

database.description: Every Eloquent / DB query → db span with statement (redacted PII), rows_affected, duration.

queue.description: Every dispatched job → producer span; every executed job → consumer span linked via trace context. Cross-queue propagation via job middleware.

cache.description: Every cache read/write → span with key, hit/miss, duration.
cache.reason: High volume + noisy — opt-in per env.

view.description: Every Blade render → span with view name, duration.
view.reason: Only enable when investigating template render latency.

attribute_driven.description: Modules opt in via attributes to add custom spans, counters, histograms without importing the SDK. Discovery pass registers them at boot; runtime instrumentation is vanilla method calls.

attributes.AsSpan: Wraps a method with a manual span. Recorded automatically before/after the method body. Params: name, kind, attributes[].
attributes.AsCounter: Emits +1 counter increment on method entry (or param-controlled amount). Params: name, unit, labels[].
attributes.AsHistogram: Records method duration_ms in a histogram. Params: name, buckets, unit.
attributes.AsGauge: Read-only observable gauge; the method's return value is the current gauge reading. Called on scrape.
attributes.AsUpDownCounter: Emits an integer delta. Useful for concurrent-count tracking (in-flight requests, cache entries).
attributes.AsContext: Adds the method's return value to the current OpenTelemetry Baggage / span attributes for the duration of the call.
attributes.AsBaggage: Propagates a key/value across service / queue boundaries in the W3C Baggage header.
